question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: hashMarket / assembly collateralParamsHash] Can an unprivileged market creator exploit the assembly block in hashMarket that computes collateralParamsHash as keccak256 over the raw memory of the collateralParamsHashes array (add(collateralParamsHashes, 0x20), mul(mload(collateralParamsHashes), 0x20)), to produce a hash collision between two markets with different collateral lists if the array memory layout is non-standard, under the precondition that the assembly correctly reads the array length and data, triggering hashMarket(M1) == hashMarket(M2) for M1.collateralParams != M2.collateralParams, violating the invariant that the market hash uniquely identifies the market's collateral configuration, causing scoped impact: offer reuse across markets with different collateral risk? Proof idea: differential fuzz test asserting hashMarket(M1) != hashMarket(M2) for all M1 != M2.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping / multicall] Can an unprivileged taker use multicall to atomically execute two takes against the same offer in the same transaction, under the precondition that consumed is updated in storage (not transient) and each delegatecall in multicall sees the updated consumed value from the previous call, triggering multicall([take(O, ..., 50), take(O, ..., 50)]) where O.maxUnits=100, violating the invariant that the consumed counter correctly accumulates across multicall steps, causing scoped impact: verify multicall does NOT allow double-filling — assert consumed[maker][group] == 100 after both takes? Proof idea: unit test multicall with two partial fills of the same offer, assert total consumed == sum of fills.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping / multicall] Can an unprivileged taker use multicall to atomically execute take(O_A) and setConsumed(group, 0, maker) in the same transaction, under the precondition that setConsumed is non-decreasing (can only increase consumed), triggering multicall([take(O_A, ..., 50), setConsumed(group, 0, maker)]) where setConsumed(group, 0, maker) is a no-op since 0 < current consumed, violating the invariant that consumed is non-decreasing, causing scoped impact: verify setConsumed cannot reset consumed to allow re-filling? Proof idea: unit test multicall([take, setConsumed(0)]), assert consumed remains at post-take value.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer with maxUnits=N and consumed[maker][group]=N-1 (one unit remaining), fill the offer for exactly 1 unit, then immediately fill it again for 0 units (no-op), under the precondition that a zero-unit take passes the ConsumedUnits check (N <= N), triggering take(O, ..., 1) then take(O, ..., 0), violating the invariant that a fully-consumed offer only allows no-op takes (Certora rule fullyConsumedOfferRevertsOnNonTrivialTake), causing scoped impact: verify zero-unit takes on exhausted offers still invoke callbacks without consuming budget? Proof idea: unit test take with units=0 on fully consumed offer, assert callback is invoked and consumed unchanged.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified / EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH] Can an unprivileged taker verify that EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH = keccak256('EIP712Domain(uint256 chainId,address verifyingContract)') matches the hardcoded constant 0x47e79534..., and if there is a mismatch, exploit it to replay signatures across different domain separators, under the precondition that the domain separator is computed as keccak256(abi.encode(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, block.chainid, address(ecrecoverRatifier))), triggering isRatified with a signature from a different chain or ratifier address, violating the invariant that signatures are domain-bound, causing scoped impact: cross-chain or cross-ratifier signature replay? Proof idea: differential test asserting EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH == keccak256('EIP712Domain(uint256 chainId,address verifyingContract)').

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified] Can an unprivileged taker submit ratifierData with ecrecover returning address(0) (by crafting v, r, s such that ecrecover returns 0), under the precondition that the check require(_signer != address(0), InvalidSignature()) is present, triggering isRatified with a zero-signer signature, violating the invariant that a valid signature must recover to a non-zero address, causing scoped impact: verify the zero-address check correctly blocks null-signer forgery? Proof idea: unit test with (v=27, r=0, s=0) or other inputs that cause ecrecover to return address(0), assert InvalidSignature revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified / isAuthorized] Can an unprivileged attacker who has been authorized by the maker (isAuthorized[maker][attacker]=true) sign an offer tree root on behalf of the maker, then use that signature to fill offers the maker never intended, under the precondition that EcrecoverRatifier checks _signer == offer.maker || IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(offer.maker, _signer), triggering isRatified where _signer == attacker and isAuthorized[maker][attacker]=true, violating the invariant that authorization is coarse-grained and an authorized address has full control, causing scoped impact: authorized address fills offers the maker never signed — is this the intended behavior or an authorization scope bug? Proof idea: unit test authorized signer filling offer with arbitrary parameters, assert this matches documented coarse-authorization model.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer with maxUnits=U and consumed[maker][group]=C where C < U, fill the offer for exactly U-C units (exhausting it), then immediately submit a second take for 0 units with a different offer O_B in the same group but with maxUnits=U+1, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group]=U after the first fill and O_B.maxUnits=U+1 > U, triggering take(O_B, ..., 1) which increments consumed to U+1 <= O_B.maxUnits, violating the invariant that exhausting one offer in a group should not allow further fills via a co-group offer with higher maxUnits, causing scoped impact: overfill — total group consumption exceeds the lower offer's cap? Proof idea: state-transition test asserting consumed[maker][group] <= min(maxUnits of all filled offers in group).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: hashOffer / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with offer.reduceOnly=true and offer.buy=true (maker is buyer), fill the offer in a way that increases the maker's credit (buyerCreditIncrease > 0), under the precondition that the reduceOnly check requires buyerCreditIncrease == 0 for buy offers, triggering take(O, ..., units) where units > buyerPos.debt, violating the invariant that reduceOnly offers cannot increase the maker's credit, causing scoped impact: MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased revert — verify the reduceOnly flag is correctly bound in hashOffer and enforced in take? Proof idea: unit test reduceOnly buy offer with units > maker's debt, assert MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer with maxAssets > 0 (assets mode) and offer.buy=true, fill the offer with units=1 at a tick where buyerPrice > WAD (impossible since buyerPrice = sellerPrice + settlementFee and sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee, so buyerPrice = offerPrice), under the precondition that buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) and buyerPrice <= WAD for valid ticks, triggering take() with units chosen to maximize buyerAssets per consumed unit, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] accurately tracks buyer asset exposure, causing scoped impact: verify rounding in mulDivDown cannot cause consumed to undercount actual assets transferred? Proof idea: fuzz (units, tick, settlementFee) and assert consumed delta == actual buyerAssets transferred.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer with maxUnits=type(uint256).max and consumed[maker][group] near type(uint256).max, trigger an overflow in consumed[maker][group] += units, under the precondition that Solidity 0.8.x has checked arithmetic and the addition would overflow, triggering take() with units chosen to overflow consumed, violating the invariant that consumed is non-decreasing and bounded, causing scoped impact: arithmetic overflow revert — verify overflow protection prevents consumed from wrapping? Proof idea: fuzz units near type(uint256).max - consumed, assert overflow reverts with Panic(0x11).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer in assets mode (maxAssets > 0) where buyerAssets or sellerAssets computation overflows uint256 (units * buyerPrice > type(uint256).max), trigger an overflow in the consumed update consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets, under the precondition that mulDivDown/mulDivUp use FullMath-style overflow-safe arithmetic, triggering take() with very large units and high tick price, violating the invariant that consumed accurately tracks assets without overflow, causing scoped impact: arithmetic overflow revert or silent truncation? Proof idea: fuzz (units, tick) near overflow boundary, assert mulDivDown/mulDivUp revert or return correct value.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: hashOffer / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O1 with offer.group=G1 and a second offer O2 with offer.group=G2 where G1 != G2 but both are signed by the same maker, fill O1 partially and then fill O2 fully, under the precondition that consumed[maker][G1] and consumed[maker][G2] are independent counters, triggering take(O1, ..., 50) then take(O2, ..., 100), violating the invariant that group isolation is maintained (fills in G2 do not affect G1's budget), causing scoped impact: verify group isolation — consumed[maker][G1] remains 50 and consumed[maker][G2] reaches 100? Proof idea: unit test two offers with different groups, assert consumed counters are independent.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with offer.expiry = block.timestamp (boundary), fill the offer at exactly the expiry timestamp, under the precondition that the check is require(block.timestamp <= offer.expiry, OfferExpired()), triggering take() at block.timestamp == offer.expiry, violating the invariant that expired offers cannot be filled, causing scoped impact: verify the expiry boundary is inclusive (<=) and does not allow fills at expiry+1? Proof idea: unit test take at expiry, expiry+1, assert OfferExpired at expiry+1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with offer.start = block.timestamp + 1 (not yet started), fill the offer before it starts, under the precondition that the check is require(block.timestamp >= offer.start, OfferNotStarted()), triggering take() at block.timestamp < offer.start, violating the invariant that offers cannot be filled before their start time, causing scoped impact: verify OfferNotStarted correctly blocks early fills? Proof idea: unit test take before start, assert OfferNotStarted revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with offer.market.maturity = block.timestamp (boundary), fill a sell offer (sellerDebtIncrease > 0) at exactly the maturity timestamp, under the precondition that the check is require(block.timestamp <= offer.market.maturity || sellerDebtIncrease == 0, CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity()), triggering take() at block.timestamp == offer.market.maturity with sellerDebtIncrease > 0, violating the invariant that debt cannot increase after maturity, causing scoped impact: verify the maturity boundary is inclusive (<=) and does not allow debt increase at maturity+1? Proof idea: unit test sell take at maturity, maturity+1, assert CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity at maturity+1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: hashOffer / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O signed for market M with enterGate=G, fill the offer in a way that bypasses the enter gate check by using a different market M' with enterGate=address(0) but the same hashMarket output, under the precondition that hashMarket includes enterGate in the hash, triggering take() with offer.market.enterGate=address(0) but the ratifier verifying against a root signed for M with enterGate=G, violating the invariant that the offer hash binds the exact gate configuration, causing scoped impact: gate bypass — taker enters a gated market without gate approval? Proof idea: verify hashMarket includes enterGate field, differential test hashMarket(M{enterGate=G}) != hashMarket(M{enterGate=0}).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with maxUnits=100 and consumed[maker][group]=99, fill the offer for 2 units (which would set consumed to 101 > 100), under the precondition that the check is require(newConsumed <= offer.maxUnits, ConsumedUnits()), triggering take(O, ..., 2) which reverts, then fill for 1 unit (consumed=100, passes), then attempt to fill for 1 more unit (consumed=101 > 100, reverts), violating the invariant that the offer is exactly exhausted at maxUnits and no further fills are possible, causing scoped impact: verify ConsumedUnits correctly enforces the cap at maxUnits? Proof idea: state-transition test at consumed=maxUnits-1, assert fill of 2 reverts and fill of 1 succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / isLeaf] Can an unprivileged taker, given a SetterRatifier where the maker has ratified root R = hashNode(hashOffer(O_A), hashOffer(O_B)), submit ratifierData with root=R, leafIndex=0, proof=[hashOffer(O_B)] to fill O_A, then submit ratifierData with root=R, leafIndex=1, proof=[hashOffer(O_A)] to fill O_B, under the precondition that both proofs are valid and the consumed counter is shared at (maker, group), triggering sequential takes that together exhaust the group budget, violating the invariant that the group budget is correctly shared across all offers in the Merkle tree, causing scoped impact: verify that filling all leaves of a Merkle tree correctly exhausts the group budget without overfill? Proof idea: unit test filling all leaves of a 2-leaf tree, assert consumed[maker][group] == sum of all fills.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with maxUnits=100 in a market where the tick spacing is later reduced (allowing new ticks), fill the offer at a new tick that was not accessible when the offer was signed, under the precondition that the offer's tick must satisfy offer.tick % marketState[id].tickSpacing == 0 at take time (not at sign time), triggering take() at a new tick after tick spacing reduction, violating the invariant that the offer hash binds the exact tick and the maker's price intent, causing scoped impact: offer filled at a different price than the maker intended? Proof idea: unit test sign offer at tick T, reduce tick spacing, fill at tick T' != T, assert TickNotAccessible or verify tick is bound in hashOffer.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with maxUnits=100 and a settlement fee that increases after the offer is signed, fill the offer at the new higher settlement fee, under the precondition that the settlement fee is not part of the offer hash (it is a market-level parameter), triggering take() after fee increase where buyerAssets is higher than when the offer was signed, violating the invariant that the maker's price intent is preserved across fee changes, causing scoped impact: offer undercharged — maker receives fewer assets than intended due to fee increase? Proof idea: unit test sign offer, increase settlement fee, fill offer, assert sellerAssets matches maker's expected price.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with maxAssets > 0 (assets mode) and a settlement fee that changes between fills, exploit the fact that consumed tracks assets (which depend on the settlement fee) so that the effective units delivered per consumed asset unit changes across fills, under the precondition that the settlement fee is not locked at offer signing time, triggering take(O, ..., units1) at fee F1 then take(O, ..., units2) at fee F2 != F1, violating the invariant that the consumed counter consistently measures the maker's exposure, causing scoped impact: overfill or undercharge — total units delivered does not match what maxAssets implies at a consistent fee? Proof idea: fuzz (fee1, fee2, units1, units2) and assert total units delivered is within expected range for maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with maxUnits=100 and consumed[maker][group]=50 (partially filled), call setConsumed(group, 50, maker) (a no-op since 50 == current consumed), then fill the offer for 50 more units, under the precondition that setConsumed is non-decreasing and setting to the current value is a no-op, triggering setConsumed(group, 50, maker) then take(O, ..., 50), violating the invariant that setConsumed cannot be used to reset or manipulate the consumed counter, causing scoped impact: verify setConsumed(group, current_value, maker) is a true no-op and does not affect subsequent fills? Proof idea: unit test setConsumed to current value, then fill, assert consumed == 100 after fill.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given two offers O_A and O_B with the same (maker, group) but different markets (different maturity), fill O_A in market M1 and O_B in market M2, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] is global (not per-market) and both offers share the same group, triggering take(O_A, ..., 50) in M1 then take(O_B, ..., 50) in M2, violating the invariant that the group budget is correctly shared across markets, causing scoped impact: verify cross-market group budget sharing works correctly and cannot be exploited to overfill one market? Proof idea: unit test two offers in different markets sharing a group, assert consumed[maker][group] == 100 after both fills.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take / consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged taker, given an offer O with maxUnits=100 and consumed[maker][group]=0, fill the offer for 100 units in a single take, then attempt to fill it again for 0 units (no-op), under the precondition that a zero-unit take on a fully consumed offer passes the ConsumedUnits check (100 <= 100), triggering take(O, ..., 0) after full consumption, violating the invariant that a fully-consumed offer only allows no-op takes (Certora rule), causing scoped impact: verify zero-unit takes on fully consumed offers still invoke callbacks — is this the intended behavior per the comment 'Midnight can call the callback of offers through a no-op take, even if those offers have consumed==max'? Proof idea: unit test zero-unit take on fully consumed offer, assert callback is invoked and consumed unchanged.
